## Rockybot — Architecture Reference

**Repo:** `/home/ubuntu/src/rockybot`  
**Version (as of last update):** 1.0.1

---

### Top-Level Structure

```
/services/bot/            Main polling bot + all 5 watchers
/services/obsidian-bridge/  Vault file watcher → Quartz static site builder
/services/obsidian-headless/  Obsidian Sync daemon (remote deployments only)
/vault-scaffold/          Default vault structure + prompt templates (baked into bot image)
/docs/                    User-facing documentation
/tests/                   Unit + integration tests
/scripts/release.sh       Semver bump + CHANGELOG + git tag automation
docker-compose.yml        Production 4-service stack
docker-compose.dev.yml    Dev stack (single container, temp vault, tight budgets)
.env.example              All env vars with defaults
```

---

### Data Model

**Storage:** Markdown files with YAML frontmatter — no database. Vault directory is the single source of truth.

**Standard frontmatter fields:**
```yaml
tags: [research-request]
created: 2026-04-17
status: pending|processing|completed|error|awaiting-input
updated: 2026-04-17
sources:
  - url: https://...
    title: "Source Title"
related: ["[[wikilink]]", "[[other]]"]
publish: true|false   # Quartz publication gate
```

**Research topic layout:**
```
research/<topic>/
├── index.md            Overview, TL;DR, sub-page list (publish: true here gates Quartz)
├── options-comparison.md
├── tradeoffs.md
└── ...other sub-pages
```

**Note-to-note links:** Obsidian wikilink format `[[research/topic/page|display text]]` — file-path relative to vault root. No separate ID system.

**Callout syntax (inline task triggers):**
```
> [!claude]    → amend-watcher: targeted inline edit
> [!expand]    → expand-watcher: create new sub-page
> [!revise]    → revise-watcher: corpus-wide rewrite of topic
```

---

### Services / Docker Stack

| Service | Image | Role | Port |
|---|---|---|---|
| `rockybot` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot` | Main polling bot, all Claude tasks | internal |
| `rockybot-bridge` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge` | Vault watcher → Quartz build | internal |
| `notes-web` | `nginx:alpine` | Serves built Quartz static site | 8080 |
| `rockybot-headless` | `ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless` | Obsidian Sync daemon (remote only) | internal |

**Shared volumes:**
- `./data/vault:/vault` — vault (shared between bot, bridge, headless)
- `./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output` — built site (shared between bridge, notes-web)

---

### Bot Watchers

All watchers serialized by global mutex in `index.js`. Serial execution order: inbox → amend → expand → revise → lint.

| Watcher | File | Trigger | Budget | Model |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **inbox** | `inbox-watcher.js` | `research/inbox/*.md` with `status: pending` | $2.00 | sonnet |
| **amend** | `amend-watcher.js` | `> [!claude]` callout anywhere | $0.50 | haiku |
| **expand** | `expand-watcher.js` | `> [!expand]` callout | $1.00 | sonnet |
| **revise** | `revise-watcher.js` | `> [!revise]` in topic `index.md` | $4.00 | sonnet |
| **lint** | `lint-watcher.js` | `status: pending` in `research/lint-trigger.md` | $5.00 | sonnet |

Inbox watcher runs a **Karpathy ingest pass** as its final step: Claude re-reads existing research tree and adds backlinks from new topic to related existing pages.

---

### Claude Integration

**No REST API.** Claude invoked as subprocess via `claude --print <prompt>`.

- **Runner:** `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — spawns CLI, collects stdout/stderr, never rejects
- **Billing:** `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` (default) or `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=true` (subscription first, API fallback)
- **Interactive auth:** If subscription fails and `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=true`, posts OAuth URL to Discord as button; 5-min timeout before fallback
- **Budget:** `--max-budget-usd` flag (API-key mode only; subscription mode ignores budget flag)
- **Prompts:** Loaded from vault `research/*-prompt.md`; user-editable in Obsidian; hardcoded fallbacks if missing

---

### Notes-Web / Quartz Frontend

**Not a dynamic web app.** Pre-rendered static HTML by Quartz v4.4.0.

- **Bridge** (`services/obsidian-bridge/src/`) watches vault with chokidar (5s poll, 3s write-debounce), runs Quartz build on change
- **Publish gate:** Only topics with `publish: true` in their `index.md` are included in build
- **Link rendering:** Wikilinks `[[topic/page]]` → relative HTML links `<a href="/topic/page/">`
- **Features:** Full-text search, backlink graph, sidebar navigation (Quartz defaults)
- **nginx config:** `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`, serves `/quartz-output/` on port 8080, 7-day cache on static assets

---

### Key File Paths

**Bot core:**
- `services/bot/src/index.js` — main loop, global mutex, watcher orchestration
- `services/bot/src/claude-runner.js` — Claude subprocess + auth fallback
- `services/bot/src/notifier.js` — Discord/Twilio notifications
- `services/bot/src/discord-bot.js` — Discord WebSocket bot (interactive auth)
- `services/bot/src/login-runner.js` — `claude login` OAuth URL capture

**Watchers:**
- `services/bot/src/inbox-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/amend-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/expand-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/revise-watcher.js`
- `services/bot/src/lint-watcher.js`

**Bridge:**
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` — chokidar watcher setup
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` — Quartz build + publish gate
- `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`

**Vault scaffold (user-editable prompts):**
- `vault-scaffold/research/research-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/amend-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/expand-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/revise-prompt.md`
- `vault-scaffold/research/lint-prompt.md`

**Docs (human-facing):**
- `docs/INSTALLATION.md`, `docs/WORKFLOW.md`, `docs/CONFIGURATION.md`, `docs/NOTIFICATIONS.md`, `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md`
- `CHANGELOG.md`

---

### Release Process

1. `./scripts/release.sh` on `main` — shows commits since last tag, proposes semver bump
2. User confirms → script updates `services/bot/package.json` version + `CHANGELOG.md`, creates git tag
3. Push tag → GitHub Actions builds & pushes images to GHCR
4. Image tags: `latest`, `vX.Y.Z`, `vX.Y`
5. `dev` branch → `edge`/`dev` images (pre-release)

**Conventional commits:** `feat:` → minor, `fix:` → patch, `feat!:`/`fix!:` → major, `docs:`/`chore:`/`test:` → no bump

---

### Key Design Decisions

- **No database:** Vault files are source of truth; direct Obsidian access
- **Global mutex:** Prevents concurrent Claude runs + vault conflicts
- **File-based state:** Status in frontmatter survives restarts/crashes
- **Prompts in vault:** User-customizable without redeploying
- **Subscription + API fallback:** Layer budget strategies
- **Quartz static site:** Zero-cost hosting; opt-in publish flag per topic
- **Obsidian Sync (not LiveSync):** LiveSync conflict resolution breaks status frontmatter fields

lines: +Published topics can be downloaded as self-contained HTML ZIPs from the notes-web UI.
+**Flow:** Browser clicks "⬇ Export ZIP" on root index → `GET /export/<slug>` → nginx proxies to bridge:3001 → bridge validates, generates ZIP in memory, streams download.
+1. Slug format: must match `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/` — blocks path traversal before any filesystem access
+2. Publish whitelist: `getPublishedTopics(vaultPath)` called fresh on every request (never cached); slug must be in published list
+4. Concurrency guard: 429 if already building same topic

+- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation: walks topic HTML, rewrites paths via cheerio (static assets → relative, within-topic links → relative, cross-topic links → neutered with tooltip), includes Quartz `static/` dir
+- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node `http` server on port 3001 with all security gates; calls `buildTopicExport` on valid requests
+**nginx:** `/export/` location proxies to `http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/`; bridge exposes port 3001 internally (`expose: ["3001"]` in docker-compose)
**Not a dynamic web app.** Pre-rendered static HTML by Quartz v4.4.0.

input.new_string:
### Topic Export

Published topics can be downloaded as self-contained HTML ZIPs from the notes-web UI.

**Flow:** Browser clicks "⬇ Export ZIP" on root index → `GET /export/<slug>` → nginx proxies to bridge:3001 → bridge validates, generates ZIP in memory, streams download.

**Security gates (in order):**
1. Slug format: must match `/^[a-z0-9-]+$/` — blocks path traversal before any filesystem access
2. Publish whitelist: `getPublishedTopics(vaultPath)` called fresh on every request (never cached); slug must be in published list
3. Quartz output must exist (`quartzOutput/<slug>/` dir)
4. Concurrency guard: 429 if already building same topic

**New files:**
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation: walks topic HTML, rewrites paths via cheerio (static assets → relative, within-topic links → relative, cross-topic links → neutered with tooltip), includes Quartz `static/` dir
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — Node `http` server on port 3001 with all security gates; calls `buildTopicExport` on valid requests

**New dependencies:** `archiver@^7`, `cheerio@^1` in `obsidian-bridge/package.json`

**nginx:** `/export/` location proxies to `http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/`; bridge exposes port 3001 internally (`expose: ["3001"]` in docker-compose)

---

### Notes-Web / Quartz Frontend

content.text:
Everything is implemented. Here's a summary of what changed:

**New files:**
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` — ZIP generation with cheerio-based HTML rewriting (static asset paths → relative, within-topic links → relative, cross-topic links → neutered)
- `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` — HTTP server on port 3001 with 4-layer security gate (format check → publish whitelist → output exists → concurrency guard)

**Updated files:**
- `package.json` — added `archiver@^7`, `cheerio@^1`
- `quartz-builder.js` — exports `getPublishedTopics`; root index now includes `· [⬇ Export ZIP](/export/<topic>)` links
- `index.js` — starts export server before initial build
- `nginx.conf` — `/export/` proxied to `bridge:3001`
- `docker-compose.yml` — bridge exposes port 3001 internally
- `docs/WORKFLOW.md` — export section added
- `CHANGELOG.md` — `[Unreleased]` entry added
- Architecture reference memory updated

To test: rebuild the bridge image (`docker build services/obsidian-bridge/`) and spin up the stack. The export link appears on the root index page next to each published topic.

content.text:
You're already on the `dev` branch in rockybot, and the homelab is already running `edge` images (switched in that recent commit). The path to get this live is clean and direct:

1. **Commit + push to rockybot `dev`** → GitHub Actions builds new `edge` images and pushes to GHCR
2. **Wait for that CI to finish** (the bridge image needs to rebuild with `archiver`/`cheerio` installed)
3. **Redeploy the homelab** → `deploy-direct.sh` pulls the new `edge` images and restarts the bridge + notes-web containers

The `/redeploy` skill handles step 3. Steps 1–2 need to happen in the rockybot repo first.

Want me to commit and push the rockybot changes now? The commit will hit `origin/dev` which triggers the image build.

Clicking it generates and downloads a self-contained HTML ZIP (topic pages + Quartz CSS/JS assets) that works offline. Cross-topic links are neutered with a tooltip. Export is on-demand via an HTTP server in the bridge container proxied through nginx; slug validation and publish-list whitelist (read fresh on every request) prevent path traversal and access to unpublished topics.

docs/WORKFLOW.md
  @@ -155,6 +155,30 @@ Within 10 minutes, Claude audits the wiki for orphaned pages, broken wikilinks,
  +## Exporting a topic
  +
  +You can download any published topic as a self-contained ZIP for offline reading or sharing with people who don't have access to your notes server.
  +
  +**How it works:**
  +
  +On the notes-web root index (`http://notes.yourdomain/`), each published topic has a **⬇ Export ZIP** link next to it. Click the link to download the topic as a `.zip` file immediately — generation takes under a second for typical topics.
  +
  +The ZIP contains:
  +- All rendered HTML pages for the topic (index + sub-pages)
  +- Quartz CSS, JavaScript, and fonts so pages render correctly offline
  +- Cross-topic links converted to non-clickable text (the content is there, the link isn't)
  +
  +Open the extracted `index.html` in any browser — no server, no internet required.
  +
  +**What doesn't work in the ZIP:**
  +- Full-text search (requires a server to build the search index)
  +- The interactive graph view (requires JS fetches)
  +- Links to pages in other topics
  +
  +**Security:** Only published topics (`publish: true` in `index.md`) can be exported. The export endpoint validates against the current publish list on every request — revoking `publish: true` immediately blocks exports, even before the Quartz rebuild completes.
  +
  +---
  +
   ## Output structure
   
   ```
  +24 -0

services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf
  @@ -8,6 +8,12 @@ server {
  +    location /export/ {
  +        proxy_pass http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/;
  +        proxy_read_timeout 30s;
  +        proxy_buffering off;
  +    }
  +
       location ~* \.(js|css|png|jpg|svg|woff2)$ {
           expires 7d;
           add_header Cache-Control "public, immutable";
  +6 -0

services/obsidian-bridge/package.json
  @@ -4,6 +4,8 @@
  +    "archiver": "^7.0.0",
  +    "cheerio": "^1.0.0",
       "chokidar": "^3.6.0",
       "fs-extra": "^11.2.0"
     }
  +2 -0

services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js
  @@ -8,6 +8,7 @@ const path = require('path');
  +const { createExportServer } = require('./http-server');
   
   const VAULT_PATH    = process.env.VAULT_PATH    || '/vault';
   const QUARTZ_OUTPUT = process.env.QUARTZ_OUTPUT || '/quartz-output';
  @@ -44,6 +45,9 @@ async function main() {
  +  createExportServer(3001, QUARTZ_OUTPUT, VAULT_PATH);
  +  logger.info('Export server listening on :3001');
  +
     // Initial build from whatever is already in the vault
     runBuild(VAULT_PATH, QUARTZ_OUTPUT);
   
  +4 -0

services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js
  @@ -51,7 +51,10 @@ function runBuild(vaultPath, quartzOutput) {
  -    const topicLinks = topics.map(t => `- [[${t}/index|${t.replace(/-/g, ' ')}]]`).join('\n');
  +    const topicLinks = topics.map(t => {
  +      const label = t.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase());
  +      return `- [[${t}/index|${label}]]  ·  [⬇ Export ZIP](/export/${t})`;
  +    }).join('\n');
       fs.outputFileSync(
         path.join(QUARTZ_SRC, 'content', 'index.md'),
         `---\ntitle: Research\n---\n\n# Research\n\n${topicLinks}\n`
  @@ -74,4 +77,4 @@ function runBuild(vaultPath, quartzOutput) {
  -module.exports = { scheduleRebuild, runBuild };
  +module.exports = { scheduleRebuild, runBuild, getPublishedTopics };

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
feat: add on-demand topic export to notes-web

Adds a per-topic "⬇ Export ZIP" link to the notes-web root index. Clicking it
generates and streams a self-contained HTML ZIP: topic pages + Quartz static
assets, with within-topic links rewritten to relative paths and cross-topic
links neutered. Works offline — open index.html in any browser.

Implementation: bridge gains a Node HTTP server on port 3001 (internal only).
nginx proxies /export/<slug> to it. Security: slug regex gate → publish-list
whitelist (read fresh per-request from vault, never cached) → Quartz output
existence check → concurrency guard. Only published topics are exportable;
revoking publish: true blocks exports immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

---
name: Publish Docker Images

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - service: bot
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot
            dockerfile: services/bot/Dockerfile
            context: .
          - service: obsidian-bridge
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-bridge
          - service: obsidian-headless
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-headless/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-headless

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Docker metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ matrix.image }}
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}
            type=raw,value=edge,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/dev' }}

      - name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: ${{ matrix.context }}
          file: ${{ matrix.dockerfile }}
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}

      - name: Notify Discord (success)
        if: success()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

      - name: Notify Discord (failure)
        if: failure()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"❌ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} build FAILED\",\"description\":\"Ref: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":15158332,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"
name: Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  image:
    name: Docker image permissions
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Build bot image
        run: docker build -f services/bot/Dockerfile -t rockybot-test .
      - name: Verify HOME=/home/ubuntu is writable for uid 1000
        # Claude Code creates lock files and temp files in HOME. If the directory
        # is owned by root (the pre-1.0.1 behaviour), both subscription billing
        # and API key mode silently exit 0 with no output.
        run: |
          docker run --rm -u 1000:1000 -e HOME=/home/ubuntu rockybot-test \
            sh -c 'touch /home/ubuntu/.write-test && rm /home/ubuntu/.write-test && echo "PASS"'

  unit:
    name: Unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

  integration:
    name: Integration tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only run when explicitly enabled — set ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true in repo variables
    if: ${{ vars.ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install Claude CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run integration tests
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_CI }}
          CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE: 'false'
          VAULT_PATH: /tmp/ci-test-vault
          RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD: '0.10'
          AMEND_BUDGET_USD: '0.05'
        run: npm run test:integration

run: npm run test:integration

  notify:
    name: Notify Discord
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [image, unit, integration]
    if: always() && github.event_name == 'push'
    steps:
      - name: Post result
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          IMAGE_RESULT: ${{ needs.image.result }}
          UNIT_RESULT: ${{ needs.unit.result }}
          INTEGRATION_RESULT: ${{ needs.integration.result }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0

          FAILED=""
          [[ "$IMAGE_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}image "
          [[ "$UNIT_RESULT"  == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}unit "
          [[ "$INTEGRATION_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}integration"
          FAILED="${FAILED% }"  # trim trailing space

          if [[ -z "$FAILED" ]]; then
            TITLE="✅ rockybot tests passed"
            COLOR=3066993
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          else
            TITLE="❌ rockybot tests FAILED: ${FAILED}"
            COLOR=15158332
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          fi

          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"${TITLE}\",\"description\":\"${DESC}\",\"color\":${COLOR},\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

Done. Two things to note about how this behaves:

- **Tests workflow** now posts one summary embed after all jobs finish — ✅ with branch name on pass, ❌ with the failing job names on failure. Only fires on push (not PRs, since the webhook secret isn't available to fork-triggered runs).
- **Publish workflow** already posted per-service — you'll see 3 embeds land once the image builds complete for this push (bot, bridge, headless).

So for this `dev` push you should see in Discord: test result first (fast), then the 3 image embeds as each one finishes.

message.content: can you give me a definitive list of secrets that i need to set for the github actions in this env

content.text:
From both workflow files:

| Secret | Where used | Required? |
|---|---|---|
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | GHCR login in `publish.yml` | Auto-provided by GitHub, no setup needed |
| `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL` | Success/failure notifications in both workflows | Optional — notifications silently skip if unset |
| `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_CI` | Integration tests in `test.yml` | Only if you set `ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true` |

There's also one **repo variable** (not a secret): `ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS` — set to `true` to enable the integration test job. Currently off by default.

So for basic operation with Discord notifications, you just need `DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL`. You can set it with:

```
gh secret set DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL --repo mrwong/rockybot
```

It'll prompt you to paste the value without echoing it to the terminal.

7

10	    name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
11	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
12	    permissions:
13	      contents: read
14	      packages: write
15	
16	    strategy:
17	      matrix:
18	        include:
19	          - service: bot
20	            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot
21	            dockerfile: services/bot/Dockerfile
22	            context: .
23	          - service: obsidian-bridge
24	            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge
25	            dockerfile: services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile
26	            context: services/obsidian-bridge
27	          - service: obsidian-headless
28	            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless
29	            dockerfile: services/obsidian-headless/Dockerfile
30	            context: services/obsidian-headless
31	
32	    steps:
33	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
34	
35	      - name: Log in to GHCR
36	        uses: docker/login-action@v3
37	        with:
38	          registry: ghcr.io
39	          username: ${{ github.actor }}
40	          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
41	
42	      - name: Docker metadata
43	        id: meta
44	        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
45	        with:
46	          images: ${{ matrix.image }}
47	          tags: |
48	            type=ref,event=branch
49	            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
50	            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
51	            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}
52	            type=raw,value=edge,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/dev' }}
53	
54	      - name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
55	        id: build
56	        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
57	        with:
58	          context: ${{ matrix.context }}
59	          file: ${{ matrix.dockerfile }}
60	          push: true
61	          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
62	          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}
63	
64	      - name: Notify Discord (success)
65	        if: success()
66	        env:
67	          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
68	        run: |
69	          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
70	          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
71	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
72	            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"
73	
74	      - name: Notify Discord (failure)
75	        if: failure()
76	        env:
77	          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
78	        run: |
79	          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
80	          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
81	            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \

file.content:
name: Publish Docker Images

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - service: bot
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot
            dockerfile: services/bot/Dockerfile
            context: .
          - service: obsidian-bridge
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-bridge
          - service: obsidian-headless
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-headless/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-headless

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Docker metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ matrix.image }}
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}
            type=raw,value=edge,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/dev' }}

      - name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: ${{ matrix.context }}
          file: ${{ matrix.dockerfile }}
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}

      - name: Notify Discord (success)
        if: success()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

      - name: Notify Discord (failure)
        if: failure()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"❌ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} build FAILED\",\"description\":\"Ref: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":15158332,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

content.text: The secret is present (`***`) and curl is running, but we can't see if Discord is rejecting it because of `-s -o /dev/null`. Let me add the HTTP status code to the log so we can see what Discord actually returns.

content.content:
1	name: Tests
2	
3	on:
4	  push:
5	    branches: [main, dev]
6	  pull_request:
7	
8	jobs:
9	  image:
10	    name: Docker image permissions
11	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
12	    steps:
13	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
14	      - name: Build bot image
15	        run: docker build -f services/bot/Dockerfile -t rockybot-test .
16	      - name: Verify HOME=/home/ubuntu is writable for uid 1000
17	        # Claude Code creates lock files and temp files in HOME. If the directory
18	        # is owned by root (the pre-1.0.1 behaviour), both subscription billing
19	        # and API key mode silently exit 0 with no output.
20	        run: |
21	          docker run --rm -u 1000:1000 -e HOME=/home/ubuntu rockybot-test \
22	            sh -c 'touch /home/ubuntu/.write-test && rm /home/ubuntu/.write-test && echo "PASS"'
23	
24	  unit:
25	    name: Unit tests
26	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
27	    steps:
28	      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
29	      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
30	        with:
31	          node-version: '20'

file.content:
name: Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  image:
    name: Docker image permissions
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Build bot image
        run: docker build -f services/bot/Dockerfile -t rockybot-test .
      - name: Verify HOME=/home/ubuntu is writable for uid 1000
        # Claude Code creates lock files and temp files in HOME. If the directory
        # is owned by root (the pre-1.0.1 behaviour), both subscription billing
        # and API key mode silently exit 0 with no output.
        run: |
          docker run --rm -u 1000:1000 -e HOME=/home/ubuntu rockybot-test \
            sh -c 'touch /home/ubuntu/.write-test && rm /home/ubuntu/.write-test && echo "PASS"'

  unit:
    name: Unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

  integration:
    name: Integration tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only run when explicitly enabled — set ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true in repo variables
    if: ${{ vars.ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install Claude CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run integration tests
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_CI }}
          CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE: 'false'
          VAULT_PATH: /tmp/ci-test-vault
          RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD: '0.10'
          AMEND_BUDGET_USD: '0.05'
        run: npm run test:integration

  notify:
    name: Notify Discord
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [image, unit, integration]
    if: always() && github.event_name == 'push'
    steps:
      - name: Post result
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          IMAGE_RESULT: ${{ needs.image.result }}
          UNIT_RESULT: ${{ needs.unit.result }}
          INTEGRATION_RESULT: ${{ needs.integration.result }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0

          FAILED=""
          [[ "$IMAGE_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}image "
          [[ "$UNIT_RESULT"  == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}unit "
          [[ "$INTEGRATION_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}integration"
          FAILED="${FAILED% }"  # trim trailing space

          if [[ -z "$FAILED" ]]; then
            TITLE="✅ rockybot tests passed"
            COLOR=3066993
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          else
            TITLE="❌ rockybot tests FAILED: ${FAILED}"
            COLOR=15158332
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          fi

          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"${TITLE}\",\"description\":\"${DESC}\",\"color\":${COLOR},\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

name: Publish Docker Images

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - service: bot
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot
            dockerfile: services/bot/Dockerfile
            context: .
          - service: obsidian-bridge
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-bridge
          - service: obsidian-headless
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-headless/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-headless

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Docker metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ matrix.image }}
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}
            type=raw,value=edge,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/dev' }}

      - name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: ${{ matrix.context }}
          file: ${{ matrix.dockerfile }}
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}

      - name: Notify Discord (success)
        if: success()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

      - name: Notify Discord (failure)
        if: failure()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          curl -s -o /dev/null -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"❌ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} build FAILED\",\"description\":\"Ref: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":15158332,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

lines: -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

-d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"❌ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} build FAILED\",\"description\":\"Ref: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":15158332,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Publish Docker Images

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - service: bot
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot
            dockerfile: services/bot/Dockerfile
            context: .
          - service: obsidian-bridge
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-bridge
          - service: obsidian-headless
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-headless/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-headless

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Docker metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ matrix.image }}
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}
            type=raw,value=edge,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/dev' }}

      - name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: ${{ matrix.context }}
          file: ${{ matrix.dockerfile }}
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}

      - name: Notify Discord (success)
        if: success()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

      - name: Notify Discord (failure)
        if: failure()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"❌ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} build FAILED\",\"description\":\"Ref: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":15158332,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  image:
    name: Docker image permissions
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Build bot image
        run: docker build -f services/bot/Dockerfile -t rockybot-test .
      - name: Verify HOME=/home/ubuntu is writable for uid 1000
        # Claude Code creates lock files and temp files in HOME. If the directory
        # is owned by root (the pre-1.0.1 behaviour), both subscription billing
        # and API key mode silently exit 0 with no output.
        run: |
          docker run --rm -u 1000:1000 -e HOME=/home/ubuntu rockybot-test \
            sh -c 'touch /home/ubuntu/.write-test && rm /home/ubuntu/.write-test && echo "PASS"'

  unit:
    name: Unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

  integration:
    name: Integration tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only run when explicitly enabled — set ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true in repo variables
    if: ${{ vars.ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install Claude CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run integration tests
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_CI }}
          CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE: 'false'
          VAULT_PATH: /tmp/ci-test-vault
          RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD: '0.10'
          AMEND_BUDGET_USD: '0.05'
        run: npm run test:integration

  notify:
    name: Notify Discord
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [image, unit, integration]
    if: always() && github.event_name == 'push'
    steps:
      - name: Post result
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          IMAGE_RESULT: ${{ needs.image.result }}
          UNIT_RESULT: ${{ needs.unit.result }}
          INTEGRATION_RESULT: ${{ needs.integration.result }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0

          FAILED=""
          [[ "$IMAGE_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}image "
          [[ "$UNIT_RESULT"  == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}unit "
          [[ "$INTEGRATION_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}integration"
          FAILED="${FAILED% }"  # trim trailing space

          if [[ -z "$FAILED" ]]; then
            TITLE="✅ rockybot tests passed"
            COLOR=3066993
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          else
            TITLE="❌ rockybot tests FAILED: ${FAILED}"
            COLOR=15158332
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          fi

          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"${TITLE}\",\"description\":\"${DESC}\",\"color\":${COLOR},\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")
          echo "Discord HTTP $HTTP_STATUS: $(cat /tmp/discord-resp)"

content.text: 302 means curl isn't following the redirect — Discord is probably redirecting from `discordapp.com` to `discord.com` (old vs new domain). Add `-L` to follow redirects.

name: Publish Docker Images

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - service: bot
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot
            dockerfile: services/bot/Dockerfile
            context: .
          - service: obsidian-bridge
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-bridge
          - service: obsidian-headless
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-headless/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-headless

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Docker metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ matrix.image }}
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}
            type=raw,value=edge,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/dev' }}

      - name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: ${{ matrix.context }}
          file: ${{ matrix.dockerfile }}
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}

      - name: Notify Discord (success)
        if: success()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")
          echo "Discord HTTP $HTTP_STATUS: $(cat /tmp/discord-resp)"

      - name: Notify Discord (failure)
        if: failure()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"❌ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} build FAILED\",\"description\":\"Ref: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":15158332,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")
          echo "Discord HTTP $HTTP_STATUS: $(cat /tmp/discord-resp)"

lines: -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")

-d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"❌ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} build FAILED\",\"description\":\"Ref: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":15158332,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")

content.text:
That's not Discord — that's a CloudFront distribution rejecting the POST. The webhook URL stored in the secret isn't a Discord webhook URL. A Discord webhook URL looks like:

```
https://discord.com/api/webhooks/<id>/<token>
```

You'll need to grab the correct one from Discord: **Server Settings → Integrations → Webhooks → Copy Webhook URL**, then update the secret:

```
gh secret set DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL --repo mrwong/rockybot
```

content.content:
- /home/ubuntu/.claude.json:/home/node/.claude.json
      - /home/ubuntu/docker-volumes/obsidian-vault:/vault
    networks:
      - traefik_network

  # Notes Web - nginx serving the Quartz-built static site
  notes-web:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: notes-web
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - /mnt/nfs/MainStoragePool/docker_data/obsidian/quartz-output:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
      - ./services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.rule=Host(`notes.${DOMAIN_NAME}`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.tls.certresolver=letsencrypt"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.middlewares=authelia@file"
      - "traefik.http.services.notes-web.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-bridge
    networks:
      - traefik_network

 # Dozzle - Real-time log viewer for Docker containers

    image: amir20/dozzle:latest

    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - DOZZLE_AUTH_PROVIDER=simple
      - DOZZLE_AUTH_TTL=48h
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro

content.text: Found it. The nginx.conf is mounted from `./services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` relative to the boughtmyowncloud docker-compose — that's a **separate copy** at `machines/dockercompute/docker/services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`. We only updated the one in the rockybot repo. Let me check and update the boughtmyowncloud copy:

content.text:
Pushed to boughtmyowncloud `main` — GitOps will pick it up automatically and redeploy, which will force-recreate the notes-web container with the updated nginx.conf. Give it a couple minutes then try `https://notes.boughtmyown.cloud/export/solar-california-oakland` again.

To answer your earlier observation: you were right, the container names in boughtmyowncloud aren't prefixed with `rockybot` — the bridge is just `obsidian-bridge` there, which is why the proxy_pass hostname in the rockybot template (`rockybot-bridge`) would have also failed even if the nginx.conf had been right.

attachment.stdout: {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecisionReason":"RTK auto-rewrite","updatedInput":{"command":"rtk read /home/ubuntu/src/rockybot/docker-compose.yml","description":"Read rockybot reference docker-compose"}}}

content.content:
services:
  # Obsidian Sync daemon — keeps the vault directory in sync with Obsidian Sync cloud
  # Requires one-time interactive setup: see docs/OBSIDIAN_SETUP.md
  obsidian-headless:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless:latest
    container_name: rockybot-headless
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./data/vault:/vault
      - ./data/obsidian-config:/config

  # Obsidian Bridge — watches vault for changes and builds a Quartz static site
  obsidian-bridge:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge:latest
    container_name: rockybot-bridge
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - QUARTZ_OUTPUT=/quartz-output
      - DOMAIN_NAME=${DOMAIN_NAME:-localhost}
    volumes:
      - ./data/vault:/vault
      - ./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output
    expose:
      - "3001"
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-headless

  # Notes Web — nginx serving the Quartz-built static site
  notes-web:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: rockybot-notes
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - ./data/quartz-output:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
      - ./services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-bridge

  # rockybot — watches vault inbox and callouts, calls Claude to process them
  bot:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot:latest
    container_name: rockybot
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - HOME=/home/ubuntu
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=${CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE:-false}
      - DRY_RUN=${DRY_RUN:-false}

      # Polling intervals (seconds)
      - INBOX_POLL_SECONDS=${INBOX_POLL_SECONDS:-600}

      # Spending caps per watcher invocation
      - RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD=${RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD:-2.00}
      - AMEND_BUDGET_USD=${AMEND_BUDGET_USD:-0.50}
      - EXPAND_BUDGET_USD=${EXPAND_BUDGET_USD:-1.00}
      - REVISE_BUDGET_USD=${REVISE_BUDGET_USD:-4.00}
      - LINT_BUDGET_USD=${LINT_BUDGET_USD:-5.00}

      # Claude model selection per watcher
      - AMEND_MODEL=${AMEND_MODEL:-haiku}
      - EXPAND_MODEL=${EXPAND_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - REVISE_MODEL=${REVISE_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - LINT_MODEL=${LINT_MODEL:-sonnet}

      # Notifications (optional — leave blank to disable)
      - DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL=${DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL:-}
      - TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=${TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID:-}
      - TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=${TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN:-}
      - TWILIO_FROM=${TWILIO_FROM:-}
      - TWILIO_TO=${TWILIO_TO:-}
    volumes:
      # Optional: mount Claude subscription session for Pro/Max billing
      # Comment out if using API key billing only (CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=false)
      - ~/.claude:/home/ubuntu/.claude
      - ./data/vault:/vault
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-headless

      - REVISE_MODEL=${REVISE_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - LINT_MODEL=${LINT_MODEL:-sonnet}

      # Notifications (optional — leave blank to disable)
      - DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL=${DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL:-}
      - TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=${TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID:-}
      - TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=${TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN:-}
      - TWILIO_FROM=${TWILIO_FROM:-}
      - TWILIO_TO=${TWILIO_TO:-}
    volumes:
      # Optional: mount Claude subscription session for Pro/Max billing
      # Comment out if using API key billing only (CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=false)
      - ~/.claude:/home/ubuntu/.claude
      - ./data/vault:/vault
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-headless

  obsidian-bridge:

  # Research Bot - polls vault inbox and [!claude] callouts, runs Claude headlessly
  # Image: github.com/mrwong/rockybot
  research-bot:

      - AMEND_POLL_SECONDS=600

      - AMEND_BUDGET_USD=0.50
      - EXPAND_BUDGET_USD=1.00
      - REVISE_BUDGET_USD=4.00
      - LINT_BUDGET_USD=5.00

      - EXPAND_MODEL=sonnet
      - REVISE_MODEL=sonnet
      - LINT_MODEL=sonnet

    volumes:

      - /home/ubuntu/.claude.json:/home/node/.claude.json
      - /home/ubuntu/docker-volumes/obsidian-vault:/vault
    networks:
      - traefik_network

  # Notes Web - nginx serving the Quartz-built static site
  notes-web:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: notes-web
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - /mnt/nfs/MainStoragePool/docker_data/obsidian/quartz-output:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
      - ./services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.rule=Host(`notes.${DOMAIN_NAME}`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.tls.certresolver=letsencrypt"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.middlewares=authelia@file"
      - "traefik.http.services.notes-web.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-bridge
    networks:
      - traefik_network

 # Dozzle - Real-time log viewer for Docker containers
  dozzle:
    image: amir20/dozzle:latest
    container_name: dozzle
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - DOZZLE_AUTH_PROVIDER=simple
      - DOZZLE_AUTH_TTL=48h
    volumes:
      - /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/docker.sock:ro

lastPrompt: can we do a comparison of the configurations expected by the rockybot setup instructions and how it's supposed to be in the homelab?

content.text:
Here's the full comparison:

| Area | Rockybot reference | Homelab | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Image tags** | `latest` | `edge` | Intentional |
| **Container names** | `rockybot-headless`, `rockybot-bridge`, `rockybot` | `obsidian-headless`, `obsidian-bridge`, `research-bot` | Cosmetic, but proxy_pass hostnames must match service name |
| **Bot `HOME`** | `/home/ubuntu` | `/home/node` | Homelab is correct — node user's actual home. Reference was never updated after the 1.0.1 Dockerfile fix |
| **Bot `CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE` default** | `false` | `true` | Intentional for homelab |
| **Claude auth mounts** | `~/.claude:/home/ubuntu/.claude` | `/home/ubuntu/.claude:/home/node/.claude` + `/home/ubuntu/.claude.json:/home/node/.claude.json` | Homelab mounts both `.claude/` and `.claude.json` — reference only mounts the directory |
| **Discord interactive auth env vars** | ❌ Missing from reference | ✅ Present | `DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH`, `DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN`, `DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID`, `DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES` all absent from reference `docker-compose.yml` |
| **`expose: ["3001"]` on bridge** | ✅ Added by us today | ❌ Not in homelab | Functionally doesn't matter (all containers on same network can reach each other regardless), but inconsistent |
| **Quartz output volume** | `./data/quartz-output` | `/mnt/nfs/MainStoragePool/...` | Intentional — homelab uses NFS |
| **Networking** | No network defined | `traefik_network` | Intentional — homelab uses Traefik |
| **notes-web access** | `ports: ["8080:8080"]` | Traefik labels + Authelia | Intentional |
| **`user: "1000:1000"`** | Not set | Set on all services | Homelab explicitly runs as non-root |

**Items that should be fixed in the rockybot reference `docker-compose.yml`:**

1. `HOME=/home/ubuntu` → `HOME=/home/node` on the bot service
2. Add the Discord interactive auth env vars to the bot service
3. Add `.claude.json` mount alongside the `.claude/` directory mount

Want me to make those corrections in the rockybot repo now?

file.content:
services:
  # Obsidian Sync daemon — keeps the vault directory in sync with Obsidian Sync cloud
  # Requires one-time interactive setup: see docs/OBSIDIAN_SETUP.md
  obsidian-headless:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless:latest
    container_name: rockybot-headless
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./data/vault:/vault
      - ./data/obsidian-config:/config

  # Obsidian Bridge — watches vault for changes and builds a Quartz static site
  obsidian-bridge:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge:latest
    container_name: rockybot-bridge
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - QUARTZ_OUTPUT=/quartz-output
      - DOMAIN_NAME=${DOMAIN_NAME:-localhost}
    volumes:
      - ./data/vault:/vault
      - ./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output
    expose:
      - "3001"
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-headless

  # Notes Web — nginx serving the Quartz-built static site
  notes-web:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: rockybot-notes
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - ./data/quartz-output:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
      - ./services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-bridge

  # rockybot — watches vault inbox and callouts, calls Claude to process them
  bot:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot:latest
    container_name: rockybot
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - HOME=/home/ubuntu
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=${CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE:-false}
      - DRY_RUN=${DRY_RUN:-false}

      # Polling intervals (seconds)
      - INBOX_POLL_SECONDS=${INBOX_POLL_SECONDS:-600}

      # Spending caps per watcher invocation
      - RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD=${RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD:-2.00}
      - AMEND_BUDGET_USD=${AMEND_BUDGET_USD:-0.50}
      - EXPAND_BUDGET_USD=${EXPAND_BUDGET_USD:-1.00}
      - REVISE_BUDGET_USD=${REVISE_BUDGET_USD:-4.00}
      - LINT_BUDGET_USD=${LINT_BUDGET_USD:-5.00}

      # Claude model selection per watcher
      - AMEND_MODEL=${AMEND_MODEL:-haiku}
      - EXPAND_MODEL=${EXPAND_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - REVISE_MODEL=${REVISE_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - LINT_MODEL=${LINT_MODEL:-sonnet}

      # Notifications (optional — leave blank to disable)
      - DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL=${DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL:-}
      - TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=${TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID:-}
      - TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=${TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN:-}
      - TWILIO_FROM=${TWILIO_FROM:-}
      - TWILIO_TO=${TWILIO_TO:-}
    volumes:
      # Optional: mount Claude subscription session for Pro/Max billing
      # Comment out if using API key billing only (CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=false)
      - ~/.claude:/home/ubuntu/.claude
      - ./data/vault:/vault
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-headless

# Image: github.com/mrwong/rockybot
  obsidian-bridge:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge:edge
    container_name: obsidian-bridge
    restart: unless-stopped
    user: "1000:1000"
    environment:
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - QUARTZ_OUTPUT=/quartz-output
      - DOMAIN_NAME=${DOMAIN_NAME}
    volumes:
      - /home/ubuntu/docker-volumes/obsidian-vault:/vault
      - /mnt/nfs/MainStoragePool/docker_data/obsidian/quartz-output:/quartz-output
    networks:
      - traefik_network

  # Research Bot - polls vault inbox and [!claude] callouts, runs Claude headlessly
  # Image: github.com/mrwong/rockybot
  research-bot:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot:edge
    container_name: research-bot
    restart: unless-stopped
    user: "1000:1000"
    environment:
      - HOME=/home/node
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=${CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE:-true}
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - INBOX_POLL_SECONDS=600
      - AMEND_POLL_SECONDS=600
      - LINT_POLL_SECONDS=600
      - RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD=2.00
      - AMEND_BUDGET_USD=0.50
      - EXPAND_BUDGET_USD=1.00
      - REVISE_BUDGET_USD=4.00
      - LINT_BUDGET_USD=5.00
      - AMEND_MODEL=haiku
      - EXPAND_MODEL=sonnet
      - REVISE_MODEL=sonnet
      - LINT_MODEL=sonnet
      - DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL=${DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL:-}
      - TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=${TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID:-}
      - TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=${TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN:-}
      - TWILIO_FROM=${TWILIO_FROM:-}
      - TWILIO_TO=${TWILIO_TO:-}
      - DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=${DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH:-true}
      - DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=${DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN:-}
      - DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID=${DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID:-}
      - DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=${DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES:-5}
    volumes:
      - /home/ubuntu/.claude:/home/node/.claude
      - /home/ubuntu/.claude.json:/home/node/.claude.json
      - /home/ubuntu/docker-volumes/obsidian-vault:/vault
    networks:
      - traefik_network

  # Notes Web - nginx serving the Quartz-built static site
  notes-web:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: notes-web

content.text:
Both pushed. The boughtmyowncloud GitOps will auto-deploy the updated config.

On sequencing — you're right, the pattern we ran into today should be the standard order going forward:

1. Make changes in rockybot (`dev` branch)
2. Wait for `edge` images to build
3. Update boughtmyowncloud to pick up the changes (including any homelab-specific files like nginx.conf copies)
4. Validate in homelab
5. Feed any corrections back to rockybot reference docs/compose
6. Merge rockybot `dev` → `main`, cut release

Want me to save this as a feedback memory so we follow it automatically next session?

message.content: homelab depends on successful rebuild of edge, so we need to do the rockybot to updte the gh images then do the push

input.content:
---
name: Rockybot deploy sequence — images before homelab
description: Always wait for rockybot edge images to finish building before pushing homelab changes that depend on them.
type: feedback
---

When making changes that affect rockybot Docker images, follow this sequence strictly:

1. Push changes to rockybot `dev` branch
2. Wait for GitHub Actions to build and push new `edge` images to GHCR (watch the Actions run or Discord notifications)
3. Only then push to boughtmyowncloud `main` to trigger the homelab deployment

**Why:** The homelab runs `docker compose pull` on deploy, which pulls whatever is currently tagged `edge` in GHCR. If you push to boughtmyowncloud before the new images are built, the homelab deploys with the old images and the new code is silently absent.

**How to apply:** Before pushing to boughtmyowncloud, check that the rockybot Actions run that built the relevant changes has completed successfully. The Discord notifications in the rockybot channel confirm each image build.

**Exception:** Changes to boughtmyowncloud-only files (nginx.conf copies, Traefik config, non-rockybot services) can be pushed independently — they don't depend on image rebuilds.

Good news — the 429 means the request is reaching the bridge correctly. The in-memory lock got stuck, likely from a client disconnect during an earlier attempt (browser navigated away or timed out before the ZIP finished, so `res.finish` never fired to release the lock).

Two things to fix: handle client disconnects in the server, and the container restart from the boughtmyowncloud push should have already cleared the lock. Let me check, then fix the disconnect handling:

2. Key Technical Concepts:

   - Security: slug whitelist via `getPublishedTopics()` called fresh on every request
   - GitHub Actions: test + publish workflows, Discord webhook notifications via curl
   - Homelab: boughtmyowncloud manages rockybot containers via its own docker-compose; has its own nginx.conf copy; uses `obsidian-bridge` as service name (not `rockybot-bridge`)

     - Key fix: `req.on('close', () => inProgress.delete(rawSlug))` to release lock on client disconnect
     ```javascript

     req.on('close', () => inProgress.delete(rawSlug));
     try {
