---
name: Rockybot deploy sequence — images before homelab
description: Always wait for rockybot edge images to finish building before pushing homelab changes that depend on them.
type: feedback
originSessionId: 8b6934c1-4c6d-4f87-962d-95658ad400d5
---
When making changes that affect rockybot Docker images, follow this sequence strictly:

1. Push changes to rockybot `dev` branch
2. Wait for GitHub Actions to build and push new `edge` images to GHCR (watch the Actions run or Discord notifications)
3. Only then push to boughtmyowncloud `main` to trigger the homelab deployment

**Why:** The homelab runs `docker compose pull` on deploy, which pulls whatever is currently tagged `edge` in GHCR. If you push to boughtmyowncloud before the new images are built, the homelab deploys with the old images and the new code is silently absent.

**How to apply:** Before pushing to boughtmyowncloud, check that the rockybot Actions run that built the relevant changes has completed successfully. The Discord notifications in the rockybot channel confirm each image build.

**Exception:** Changes to boughtmyowncloud-only files (nginx.conf copies, Traefik config, non-rockybot services) can be pushed independently — they don't depend on image rebuilds.

services:
  # Obsidian Sync daemon — keeps the vault directory in sync with Obsidian Sync cloud
  # Requires one-time interactive setup: see docs/OBSIDIAN_SETUP.md
  obsidian-headless:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless:latest
    container_name: rockybot-headless
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - ./data/vault:/vault
      - ./data/obsidian-config:/config

  # Obsidian Bridge — watches vault for changes and builds a Quartz static site
  obsidian-bridge:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge:latest
    container_name: rockybot-bridge
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - QUARTZ_OUTPUT=/quartz-output
      - DOMAIN_NAME=${DOMAIN_NAME:-localhost}
    volumes:
      - ./data/vault:/vault
      - ./data/quartz-output:/quartz-output
    expose:
      - "3001"
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-headless

  # Notes Web — nginx serving the Quartz-built static site
  notes-web:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: rockybot-notes
    restart: unless-stopped
    ports:
      - "8080:8080"
    volumes:
      - ./data/quartz-output:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
      - ./services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-bridge

  # rockybot — watches vault inbox and callouts, calls Claude to process them
  bot:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot:latest
    container_name: rockybot
    restart: unless-stopped
    environment:
      - HOME=/home/node
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=${CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE:-false}
      - DRY_RUN=${DRY_RUN:-false}

      # Polling interval (seconds) — applies to all watchers
      - INBOX_POLL_SECONDS=${INBOX_POLL_SECONDS:-600}

      # Spending caps per watcher invocation
      - RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD=${RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD:-2.00}
      - AMEND_BUDGET_USD=${AMEND_BUDGET_USD:-0.50}
      - EXPAND_BUDGET_USD=${EXPAND_BUDGET_USD:-1.00}
      - REVISE_BUDGET_USD=${REVISE_BUDGET_USD:-4.00}
      - LINT_BUDGET_USD=${LINT_BUDGET_USD:-5.00}

      # Claude model selection per watcher
      - AMEND_MODEL=${AMEND_MODEL:-haiku}
      - EXPAND_MODEL=${EXPAND_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - REVISE_MODEL=${REVISE_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - LINT_MODEL=${LINT_MODEL:-sonnet}

      # Notifications (optional — leave blank to disable)
      - DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL=${DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL:-}
      - TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=${TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID:-}
      - TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=${TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN:-}
      - TWILIO_FROM=${TWILIO_FROM:-}
      - TWILIO_TO=${TWILIO_TO:-}

      # Interactive Discord auth for Claude subscription re-login (optional)
      # Requires a Discord bot token and a channel the bot can post to
      - DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=${DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH:-false}
      - DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=${DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN:-}
      - DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID=${DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID:-}
      - DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=${DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES:-5}
    volumes:
      # Optional: mount Claude subscription session for Pro/Max billing
      # Comment out if using API key billing only (CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=false)
      - ~/.claude:/home/node/.claude
      - ~/.claude.json:/home/node/.claude.json
      - ./data/vault:/vault
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-headless

attachment.planContent:
# Plan: Topic Export Feature for Rockybot

## Context

Users want to share a research topic (a Quartz-rendered tree of notes) with people who may not have access to the live notes-web server. The export must be portable and self-contained: internal links within the topic navigate between pages, links to other topics are gracefully degraded, and the download is triggered directly from the notes-web UI. The existing bridge container handles generation; nginx proxies the export request to it.

---

## Export Format: Self-Contained HTML ZIP (on-demand)

**Format:** A ZIP file per topic containing the topic's rendered HTML pages + Quartz static assets (CSS, JS, fonts), with cross-topic links neutered. Generated on-demand when the user clicks "Export" — not pre-built.

**Why on-demand instead of pre-generated:**
- Topic trees are small; ZIP generation is fast (sub-second for typical topics)
- No stale ZIPs sitting around; always reflects the latest build
- No extra disk space overhead

**Cross-topic links:** `href` attributes pointing outside the topic are removed; link text remains with `title="External topic (not included in export)"`.

---

## Architecture: How the Request Flows

```
Browser clicks "Export ZIP" link on notes-web page
  → GET /export/<topicSlug>  (hits nginx on port 8080)
  → nginx proxy_pass to bridge:3001/export/<topicSlug>
  → bridge validates slug, generates ZIP in memory, streams as download
  → browser receives file download
```

No new containers. Bridge gains a small HTTP server on port 3001 (internal only, not exposed to host).

---

## Security Design

This is the most critical part of the feature. All protections are enforced before any file is touched:

**1. Slug whitelist (primary gate)**
The topicSlug from the URL is validated against `getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH)` — the same function already used by the build system. This returns only directory names that exist in `research/` with `publish: true`. The raw request string is never used to construct a file path. If the slug isn't in the whitelist, return 404 immediately.

```javascript
const published = getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH); // ['password-managers', 'auth-systems', ...]
if (!published.includes(requestedSlug)) { return 404; }
// Only now do we use the slug — it came from the filesystem, not the request
const topicDir = path.join(QUARTZ_OUTPUT, requestedSlug); // safe
```

**Note on whitelist freshness:** `getPublishedTopics(vaultPath)` reads the vault from disk on every export request — it is never cached. This means unpublishing a topic (`publish: true` → `false`) blocks exports immediately on the next request, before the Quartz rebuild even completes.

**2. Slug format enforcement (defense in depth)**
Even before the whitelist check, reject any slug that contains characters outside `[a-z0-9-]`. This blocks `../`, URL-encoded traversal, null bytes, and path separators before they reach any logic.

**3. File walk uses symlink-safe options**
When walking `quartzOutput/<topic>/` to collect files for the ZIP, use `fs.readdir` with explicit `withFileTypes` and skip any entries that are symlinks. Quartz output is generated by our own code and shouldn't contain symlinks, but we skip them defensively.

**4. ZIP paths are constructed from filesystem walk, not user input**
Every path added to the ZIP is derived from the directory walk of `quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/`, with the topic prefix stripped. No part of the URL request touches ZIP entry names.

**5. No access to vault, secrets, or host paths**
The export-builder only reads from `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/<topicSlug>/` and `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/static/`. These are Quartz-generated HTML — not the raw vault, not secrets, not environment variables. The bridge runs as non-root (`node` user) with no Docker socket, no privileged mode, no host paths outside the declared volumes.

**6. Concurrency guard**
If a request for `<topicSlug>` is already in progress, return 429. Prevents resource exhaustion from repeated rapid requests.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1 — Add dependencies to `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json`

```json
"archiver": "^7.0.0",
"cheerio": "^1.0.0"
```

### Step 2 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js`

Exports one async function: `buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, responseStream)`

**Logic:**

```
buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, res)
  1. Set response headers: Content-Type: application/zip,
     Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<topicSlug>-export.zip"
  2. Create archiver ZIP stream piped to res
  3. Walk all files under quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/ (no symlinks)
       For each .html file:
         - Compute depth (number of path segments relative to topic root)
         - Call rewriteHtml(content, topicSlug, depth)
         - Add to ZIP at path stripped of topic-slug prefix
           e.g. quartzOutput/password-managers/bitwarden/index.html → bitwarden/index.html
  4. Copy quartzOutput/static/ into ZIP at static/ (skip symlinks)
  5. Finalize ZIP

rewriteHtml(htmlContent, topicSlug, depth)
  Uses cheerio. prefix = "../".repeat(depth)

  a) Static asset paths (src/href starting with /static/):
     /static/foo.js → {prefix}static/foo.js

  b) Within-topic page links:
     href="/topicSlug/sub-page" → href="{prefix}sub-page/index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/index"   → href="{prefix}index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/"        → href="{prefix}index.html"

  c) Cross-topic page links:
     href starts with "/" but not with "/<topicSlug>/"
     → remove href attribute
     → add title="External topic (not included in export)"
     → add class="export-external-link"

  d) Anchor-only (#section), mailto:, relative paths → unchanged
```

### Step 3 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js`

Small Node `http` server (no Express needed):

```
createExportServer(port, quartzOutput, vaultPath)
  Listens on 0.0.0.0:port (internal Docker port, not exposed to host)

  Routes:
    GET /export/<slug>
      1. Validate slug: reject if not /^[a-z0-9-]+$/
      2. Check slug in getPublishedTopics(vaultPath) → 404 if not found
      3. Check concurrency guard → 429 if already building this slug
      4. Call buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, slug, res)

    All other paths → 404
```

### Step 4 — Wire HTTP server into `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js`

After `configureQuartz()` and before the chokidar watcher:

```javascript
const { createExportServer } = require('./http-server');
createExportServer(3001, QUARTZ_OUTPUT, VAULT_PATH);
logger.info('Export HTTP server listening on :3001');
```

### Step 5 — Update `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`

Add a proxy block inside the existing `server {}`:

```nginx
location /export/ {
    proxy_pass http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/;
    proxy_read_timeout 30s;
    proxy_buffering off;
}
```

Notes-web (nginx) and the bridge are on the same Docker network and can reach each other by service name.

### Step 6 — Add export links to the generated root index in `quartz-builder.js`

Change the `topicLinks` generation:

```javascript
// Before:
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => `- [[${t}/index|${t.replace(/-/g, ' ')}]]`).join('\n');

// After:
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => {
  const label = t.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase());
  return `- [[${t}/index|${label}]] · [⬇ Export ZIP](/export/${t})`;
}).join('\n');
```

### Step 7 — `docker-compose.yml`: expose bridge's port 3001 internally

Add to the `rockybot-bridge` service (no host port mapping needed — nginx reaches it by service name):

```yaml
rockybot-bridge:
  expose:
    - "3001"
```

---

## Critical Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json` | Add `archiver`, `cheerio` |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` | **New** — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` | **New** — HTTP API with slug validation + security gate |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` | Start HTTP server on port 3001 |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` | Add export links to root index generation |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` | Add `/export/` proxy_pass block |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Add `expose: ["3001"]` to bridge service |
| `docs/WORKFLOW.md` | Document the export feature |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Add entry |

---

## Verification

1. Start the stack locally with `docker compose up`
2. Add a topic with `publish: true`, wait for Quartz build
3. Open `http://localhost:8080` — verify a "⬇ Export ZIP" link appears next to the topic
4. Click the link — browser should immediately start downloading `<topic>-export.zip`
5. Extract the ZIP and open `index.html` in a browser (file://)
6. Verify:
   - Topic index renders with Quartz styling (CSS/JS from local `static/`)
   - Sub-page links navigate correctly between pages in the ZIP
   - Cross-topic links render as non-clickable text with tooltip
7. Security spot-checks:
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/../etc/passwd` → 404
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/unpublished-topic` → 404
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/` → 404

file.content:
# Obsidian Headless - continuous bidirectional sync with Obsidian Sync
  # Image: github.com/mrwong/rockybot
  obsidian-headless:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless:edge
    container_name: obsidian-headless
    restart: unless-stopped
    user: "1000:1000"
    volumes:
      - /home/ubuntu/docker-volumes/obsidian-vault:/vault
      - /home/ubuntu/docker-volumes/obsidian-config:/config
    networks:
      - traefik_network

  # Obsidian Bridge - watches local vault and builds Quartz static site
  # Image: github.com/mrwong/rockybot
  obsidian-bridge:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge:edge
    container_name: obsidian-bridge
    restart: unless-stopped
    user: "1000:1000"
    environment:
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - QUARTZ_OUTPUT=/quartz-output
      - DOMAIN_NAME=${DOMAIN_NAME}
    volumes:
      - /home/ubuntu/docker-volumes/obsidian-vault:/vault
      - /mnt/nfs/MainStoragePool/docker_data/obsidian/quartz-output:/quartz-output
    expose:
      - "3001"
    networks:
      - traefik_network

  # Research Bot - polls vault inbox and [!claude] callouts, runs Claude headlessly
  # Image: github.com/mrwong/rockybot
  research-bot:
    image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot:edge
    container_name: research-bot
    restart: unless-stopped
    user: "1000:1000"
    environment:
      - HOME=/home/node
      - ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=${ANTHROPIC_API_KEY}
      - CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE=${CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE:-true}
      - VAULT_PATH=/vault
      - DRY_RUN=${DRY_RUN:-false}

      # Polling interval (seconds) — applies to all watchers
      - INBOX_POLL_SECONDS=${INBOX_POLL_SECONDS:-600}

      # Spending caps per watcher invocation
      - RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD=${RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD:-2.00}
      - AMEND_BUDGET_USD=${AMEND_BUDGET_USD:-0.50}
      - EXPAND_BUDGET_USD=${EXPAND_BUDGET_USD:-1.00}
      - REVISE_BUDGET_USD=${REVISE_BUDGET_USD:-4.00}
      - LINT_BUDGET_USD=${LINT_BUDGET_USD:-5.00}

      # Claude model selection per watcher
      - AMEND_MODEL=${AMEND_MODEL:-haiku}
      - EXPAND_MODEL=${EXPAND_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - REVISE_MODEL=${REVISE_MODEL:-sonnet}
      - LINT_MODEL=${LINT_MODEL:-sonnet}

      # Notifications (optional — leave blank to disable)
      - DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL=${DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL:-}
      - TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID=${TWILIO_ACCOUNT_SID:-}
      - TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN=${TWILIO_AUTH_TOKEN:-}
      - TWILIO_FROM=${TWILIO_FROM:-}
      - TWILIO_TO=${TWILIO_TO:-}

      # Interactive Discord auth for Claude subscription re-login (optional)
      - DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH=${DISCORD_INTERACTIVE_AUTH:-true}
      - DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN=${DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN:-}
      - DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID=${DISCORD_CHANNEL_ID:-}
      - DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=${DISCORD_AUTH_TIMEOUT_MINUTES:-5}
    volumes:
      - /home/ubuntu/.claude:/home/node/.claude
      - /home/ubuntu/.claude.json:/home/node/.claude.json
      - /home/ubuntu/docker-volumes/obsidian-vault:/vault
    networks:
      - traefik_network

  # Notes Web - nginx serving the Quartz-built static site
  notes-web:
    image: nginx:alpine
    container_name: notes-web
    restart: unless-stopped
    volumes:
      - /mnt/nfs/MainStoragePool/docker_data/obsidian/quartz-output:/usr/share/nginx/html:ro
      - ./services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/conf.d/default.conf
    labels:
      - "traefik.enable=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.entrypoints=websecure"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.rule=Host(`notes.${DOMAIN_NAME}`)"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.tls=true"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.tls.certresolver=letsencrypt"
      - "traefik.http.routers.notes-web.middlewares=authelia@file"
      - "traefik.http.services.notes-web.loadbalancer.server.port=8080"
    depends_on:
      - obsidian-bridge
    networks:

content.text:
Here's where things stand:

**Feature shipped (in homelab):**
- Export ZIP feature is live — bridge runs HTTP server on port 3001, nginx proxies `/export/<slug>`
- Export links appear on the notes index page
- Discord build notifications working

**Two bugs reported just before context ran out:**

1. **403 on notes.boughtmyown.cloud** — likely transient from container restart during deploy (Authelia middleware briefly loses the upstream). Probably resolved itself.

2. **"Index files all missing from display"** — I was investigating this when you interrupted. The new topicLinks format in `quartz-builder.js` mixes a wikilink with a markdown link on the same line:
   ```
   - [[solar-california-oakland/index|Solar California Oakland]]  ·  [⬇ Export ZIP](/export/solar-california-oakland)
   ```
   This might be confusing Quartz's parser, causing the root index to render without any topic entries. I hadn't confirmed the root cause yet.

**Pending:**
- Disconnect fix (export lock stuck on client disconnect) — was pushed to rockybot `dev` but the homelab may be running the earlier edge image
- Integration tests — currently pass vacuously via `--passWithNoTests`, no real tests written yet
- Rockybot dev → main merge / release not done yet

**Next step I was about to take:** Simplify the topicLinks format to use plain markdown links instead of mixed wikilink+markdown, since that's the most likely cause of the missing index display. Want me to continue with that?

content.text:
Two separate symptoms with different causes. Let me think through them:

**Explorer "missing index"** — this is likely expected Quartz behavior. In Quartz, a folder's `index.md` isn't shown as a separate item in the explorer — the folder itself IS the index. The topic should appear as a folder, clicking it shows the index page.

**403 on direct access** — this is the real bug. A 403 (not 404) on a directory path means the directory exists on disk but nginx can't read the index.html inside it — almost certainly a file permissions issue on the NFS mount.

The most likely cause: the bridge's post-build `chmod -R o+r` is failing on the NFS mount. Let me check the bridge logs to confirm. Can you run:

```
! docker logs obsidian-bridge --tail 100 2>&1
```

I also want to verify the actual permissions on the quartz-output:

```
! ls -la /mnt/nfs/MainStoragePool/docker_data/obsidian/quartz-output/ | head -20
```

And one topic dir:

```
! ls -la /mnt/nfs/MainStoragePool/docker_data/obsidian/quartz-output/solar-california-oakland/ 2>/dev/null | head -10
```

content.text:
I can see two issues clearly now:

1. **Explorer folders are `<button>` elements, not links** — clicking just expands/collapses. Quartz's explorer behavior for folders is expand-only by default. The index page for a topic is accessible at its URL directly, not through the explorer.

2. **`/research/...` links 404** — vault notes use absolute vault-relative paths (`[[research/mach-e-acc-sensor/...]]`), but the rsync strips the `research/` prefix. The HTML ends up with `../research/mach-e-acc-sensor/known-causes-tsbs` links that resolve to `/research/...` which doesn't exist.

The fix for #2 is nginx rewrites to redirect `/research/*` → `/*`. Let me add that to both nginx configs:

lines: +    # Vault notes use research/-prefixed absolute wikilinks; redirect them to the actual topic paths

lines: +    # Vault notes use research/-prefixed absolute wikilinks; redirect them to the actual topic paths

content.text: Now let me push the boughtmyowncloud nginx.conf fix to trigger a homelab redeploy (nginx.conf is a volume mount, not in the image, so no image rebuild needed). Also push the rockybot change:

cd /home/ubuntu/src/boughtmyowncloud && git add machines/dockercompute/docker/services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: redirect /research/* paths to topic root for vault wikilink compatibility

Vault notes use vault-relative absolute paths (research/topic/page) in their
wikilinks, which Quartz resolves as /research/... URLs. These 404 because the
rsync strips the research/ prefix. Redirect transparently to the correct path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
input.description: Commit homelab nginx.conf fix

input.command:
git add services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: redirect /research/* paths to topic root for vault wikilink compatibility

Vault notes use vault-relative absolute paths (research/topic/page) in their
wikilinks, which Quartz resolves as /research/... URLs. These 404 because the
rsync strips the research/ prefix. Redirect transparently to the correct path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Good — the redirect is live. The `/research/...` links will now work correctly for authenticated users.

Now for the explorer. I spotted the root cause in the HTML:

```
data-behavior="collapse"
```

Quartz has two folder click modes: `"collapse"` (just expand/collapse) and `"link"` (navigate to folder index). It's set to `"collapse"`. We need to patch the Quartz config. Let me check where this is set:

input.description: Check Explorer config in Quartz source inside container

attachment.stdout: {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecisionReason":"RTK auto-rewrite","updatedInput":{"command":"rtk docker exec obsidian-bridge grep -n \"folderClickBehavior\\|Explorer\\|collapse\\|link\" /quartz-src/quartz.config.ts 2>/dev/null | head -20","description":"Check Explorer config in Quartz source inside container"}}}

attachment.stdout: {"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecisionReason":"RTK auto-rewrite","updatedInput":{"command":"rtk docker exec obsidian-bridge sed -n '83,95p' /quartz-src/quartz/util/path.ts 2>/dev/null","description":"Read simplifySlug implementation"}}}

content.text:
I can see the issue now. With `folderClickBehavior: "collapse"`, the topic `index.md` page ends up as a root-level leaf link (not inside the folder). Quartz's `simplifySlug("mach-e-acc-sensor/index")` produces `"mach-e-acc-sensor"`, so it appears at the root level separately from the folder.

With `folderClickBehavior: "link"`, the folder title itself becomes the link to the index page, and the chevron still expands the children. Both behaviors coexist — the user can click the title to navigate OR click the chevron to expand.

I'll patch this at runtime in `configureQuartz()` (same approach as the `baseUrl` patch):

lastPrompt: clicking on the explorer just simply opens and closes the expand, it doesn't load the index. https://notes.boughtmyown.cloud/research/mach-e-acc-sensor/ goes to 404. this agent is running on the host.…

content.content: The file /home/ubuntu/src/rockybot/services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js has been updated successfully. (file state is current in your context — no need to Read it back)

lines: +  // Patch layout: make explorer folder titles link to the folder index page.
+  // Without this, Quartz places index.md as a root-level leaf (not inside the folder)
+  // because simplifySlug strips /index, making "topic/index" → "topic" at root level.

content.text: Now commit and push to rockybot `dev`, then wait for the edge image to build before redeploying the homelab:

input.command:
cd /home/ubuntu/src/rockybot && git add services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: set Explorer folderClickBehavior to link so topic folders navigate to index

Without this, Quartz's simplifySlug strips /index from the slug, placing topic
index.md as a root-level leaf rather than inside its folder. With folderClickBehavior
"link", clicking the folder title navigates to the index; the chevron still expands.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

content.text:
Now I have everything I need. The issues are:

1. **Styling broken**: `../prescript.js`, `../postscript.js`, `../index.css` are referenced relatively from the topic dir — but the ZIP strips the `mach-e-acc-sensor/` prefix, making those paths one level wrong. Fix: keep files at `mach-e-acc-sensor/` in the ZIP.
2. **Navigation tree**: `.explorer` div gets included wholesale — need to strip it, along with `.search`.
3. **Scripts**: `prescript.js`/`postscript.js` are SPA navigation bundles that won't work offline — strip them.
4. **Within-topic links**: Quartz generates `../mach-e-acc-sensor/page` (no `.html`) — those need rewriting to `./page.html`.

lines: +// Converts a within-topic href (relative ../topicSlug/X or absolute /topicSlug/X)
+// to a same-directory relative .html path for use inside the ZIP.
-  // Remove <base> tag — we use explicit relative paths throughout
+  if (hashIdx !== -1) { fragment = rest.slice(hashIdx); rest = rest.slice(0, hashIdx); }
+  // Remove elements that don't work or make sense in an offline ZIP export
+  $('script').remove();                       // SPA routing, graph, search — all need a server
-    // Clean URL (no file extension) → Quartz page → append /index.html
-    // Has extension (image, PDF, etc.) → resource file
-    // External URLs and fragment-only links — leave unchanged
+    // External URLs, fragments, mailto → leave unchanged
-    // Cross-topic absolute links — neuter: remove href, keep text, add tooltip
+    // Everything else (cross-topic, site root, tags, search) → neuter
+      // Topic files at topicSlug/ in the ZIP — preserves the relative ../X paths
+      // that Quartz generates for root-level assets (index.css, static/)
+      // Root-level CSS — linked as ../index.css from within topicSlug/
+      // Static assets — linked as ../static/X from within topicSlug/

content.text:
CI is building. Once the edge images are published, redeploy the homelab. The summary of what this fixes:

- **Styling**: Topic HTML stays at `mach-e-acc-sensor/index.html` inside the ZIP, so `../index.css` and `../static/` resolve correctly without any path rewriting
- **Navigation tree stripped**: Removes `.explorer`, `.search`, `.graph-outer`, `.backlinks`

- **Within-topic links work**: `../mach-e-acc-sensor/known-causes-tsbs` → `./known-causes-tsbs.html`
- **Cross-topic links neutered**: anything referencing other topics or server paths gets `href` removed with a tooltip

The user opens the ZIP as `mach-e-acc-sensor/index.html` (the topic's own subfolder inside the ZIP).

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring or one-time remote agent ("run this every Monday", "open a cleanup PR for X in 2 weeks"), or to manage existing routines. ALSO OFFER PROACTIVELY: after you finish work that has a natural future follow-up, end your reply with a one-line offer to schedule a background agent to do it. Strong signals: a feature flag / gate / experiment / staged rollout was just shipped (offer a one-time agent in ~2 weeks to open a cleanup PR or evaluate results), a new alert/monitor was created (offer a recurring agent to triage it), a TODO/migration with a "remove once X" condition was left behind (offer a one-time agent to do the removal). Skip the offer for refactors, bug fixes, and anything that is done once it ships. Name a concrete action and cadence ("in 2 weeks", "every Monday") and only offer when the run just succeeded — do not pitch a schedule for something that has not happened yet.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- promote: /promote — Promote session work into the Obsidian wiki
- redeploy: Redeploy the homelab by rerunning the most recent boughtmyowncloud GitOps Sync run:
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Here's the plan:

---

## Integration Test Plan for Rockybot

### What to test

There are three independent surface areas worth covering:

---

### 1. `getPublishedTopics` — vault scanning (`tests/integration/quartz-builder.test.js`)

**What it does**: Reads `research/<topic>/index.md` files, checks `publish: true` in frontmatter, returns the list of slugs.

**Test cases using the existing `tests/fixtures/test-vault/`:**
- Returns only topics with `publish: true` (not `false`, not missing)
- Ignores directories without an `index.md`
- Returns empty array when `research/` doesn't exist
- Returns correct slug names (the directory name, not the title)

**Why integration not unit**: hits real filesystem, no mocking needed, fast.

---

### 2. `export-builder` — ZIP generation (`tests/integration/export-builder.test.js`)

**What it does**: Takes a `quartzOutput` directory, streams a ZIP to a response-like object, rewrites HTML paths.

**Setup**: Create a minimal fake `quartzOutput/` in `tests/fixtures/` with:
```
fake-quartz-output/
  my-topic/
    index.html          ← has ../index.css, ../static/icon.png, ../my-topic/sub-page, ../other-topic/page
    sub-page.html
  index.css
  static/
    icon.png
```

**Test cases:**
- ZIP contains `index.html`, `sub-page.html`, `index.css`, `static/icon.png` at the right paths
- `../index.css` → `index.css` (stripParent applied to `<link>`)
- `../static/icon.png` → `static/icon.png` (stripParent applied to `<link>`)
- `../my-topic/sub-page` → `./sub-page.html` (within-topic link rewritten)
- `../my-topic/` → `./index.html` (topic root link)
- `../other-topic/page` → href removed, class `export-external-link` (cross-topic neutered)
- Explorer div removed from output
- No `<script>` tags in output
- Response headers: `Content-Type: application/zip`, correct `Content-Disposition` filename

**Helper**: use the `archiver` + a `PassThrough` stream + `unzipper` to read ZIP contents in memory.

---

### 3. `http-server` — security gates (`tests/integration/http-server.test.js`)

**What it does**: Validates slug format, checks publish whitelist, checks Quartz output exists, enforces concurrency limit.

**Setup**: Start a real `http.Server` on a random port using `createExportServer`, with a temp dir wired as `quartzOutput` and `vaultPath`.

**Test cases:**
- `GET /export/valid-topic` → calls `buildTopicExport` (mock it), returns 200
- `GET /export/../etc/passwd` → 404 (slug format gate rejects non-`[a-z0-9-]`)
- `GET /export/UPPERCASE` → 404
- `GET /export/unpublished-topic` → 404 (not in published whitelist)
- `GET /export/valid-topic` when Quartz output dir doesn't exist → 503
- `POST /export/valid-topic` → 405
- `GET /` → 404
- Concurrency: second concurrent request for same slug → 429

**Fixtures needed**: a minimal vault with one topic (`publish: true`) and one without, and a minimal `quartzOutput/valid-topic/` directory.

---

### Implementation order

1. **`getPublishedTopics`** — simplest, no dependencies beyond the existing fixture vault. Can extend `tests/fixtures/test-vault/research/` with a few `publish: true/false` dirs.
2. **`export-builder`** — needs a fake Quartz output fixture. The HTML rewriting logic is the most complex and most likely to regress.
3. **`http-server`** — needs a real server spun up per test; mock `buildTopicExport` to avoid needing a full Quartz output.

### What's explicitly out of scope

- The actual Quartz build (`runBuild`) — requires the full Quartz toolchain baked into the Docker image; not feasible in CI without the image. Test the inputs/outputs around it, not the build itself.
- The bot watchers — already have unit tests; integration tests would require a real Claude API call.
- obsidian-headless sync — external dependency, not testable in isolation.

---

Want me to start implementing these? I'd suggest starting with `export-builder` since that's the surface area we just heavily changed.

attachment.planContent:
# Plan: Topic Export Feature for Rockybot

## Context

Users want to share a research topic (a Quartz-rendered tree of notes) with people who may not have access to the live notes-web server. The export must be portable and self-contained: internal links within the topic navigate between pages, links to other topics are gracefully degraded, and the download is triggered directly from the notes-web UI. The existing bridge container handles generation; nginx proxies the export request to it.

---

## Export Format: Self-Contained HTML ZIP (on-demand)

**Format:** A ZIP file per topic containing the topic's rendered HTML pages + Quartz static assets (CSS, JS, fonts), with cross-topic links neutered. Generated on-demand when the user clicks "Export" — not pre-built.

**Why on-demand instead of pre-generated:**
- Topic trees are small; ZIP generation is fast (sub-second for typical topics)
- No stale ZIPs sitting around; always reflects the latest build
- No extra disk space overhead

**Cross-topic links:** `href` attributes pointing outside the topic are removed; link text remains with `title="External topic (not included in export)"`.

---

## Architecture: How the Request Flows

```
Browser clicks "Export ZIP" link on notes-web page
  → GET /export/<topicSlug>  (hits nginx on port 8080)
  → nginx proxy_pass to bridge:3001/export/<topicSlug>
  → bridge validates slug, generates ZIP in memory, streams as download
  → browser receives file download
```

No new containers. Bridge gains a small HTTP server on port 3001 (internal only, not exposed to host).

---

## Security Design

This is the most critical part of the feature. All protections are enforced before any file is touched:

**1. Slug whitelist (primary gate)**
The topicSlug from the URL is validated against `getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH)` — the same function already used by the build system. This returns only directory names that exist in `research/` with `publish: true`. The raw request string is never used to construct a file path. If the slug isn't in the whitelist, return 404 immediately.

```javascript
const published = getPublishedTopics(VAULT_PATH); // ['password-managers', 'auth-systems', ...]
if (!published.includes(requestedSlug)) { return 404; }
// Only now do we use the slug — it came from the filesystem, not the request
const topicDir = path.join(QUARTZ_OUTPUT, requestedSlug); // safe
```

**Note on whitelist freshness:** `getPublishedTopics(vaultPath)` reads the vault from disk on every export request — it is never cached. This means unpublishing a topic (`publish: true` → `false`) blocks exports immediately on the next request, before the Quartz rebuild even completes.

**2. Slug format enforcement (defense in depth)**
Even before the whitelist check, reject any slug that contains characters outside `[a-z0-9-]`. This blocks `../`, URL-encoded traversal, null bytes, and path separators before they reach any logic.

**3. File walk uses symlink-safe options**
When walking `quartzOutput/<topic>/` to collect files for the ZIP, use `fs.readdir` with explicit `withFileTypes` and skip any entries that are symlinks. Quartz output is generated by our own code and shouldn't contain symlinks, but we skip them defensively.

**4. ZIP paths are constructed from filesystem walk, not user input**
Every path added to the ZIP is derived from the directory walk of `quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/`, with the topic prefix stripped. No part of the URL request touches ZIP entry names.

**5. No access to vault, secrets, or host paths**
The export-builder only reads from `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/<topicSlug>/` and `QUARTZ_OUTPUT/static/`. These are Quartz-generated HTML — not the raw vault, not secrets, not environment variables. The bridge runs as non-root (`node` user) with no Docker socket, no privileged mode, no host paths outside the declared volumes.

**6. Concurrency guard**
If a request for `<topicSlug>` is already in progress, return 429. Prevents resource exhaustion from repeated rapid requests.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Step 1 — Add dependencies to `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json`

```json
"archiver": "^7.0.0",
"cheerio": "^1.0.0"
```

### Step 2 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js`

Exports one async function: `buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, responseStream)`

**Logic:**

```
buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, topicSlug, res)
  1. Set response headers: Content-Type: application/zip,
     Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="<topicSlug>-export.zip"
  2. Create archiver ZIP stream piped to res
  3. Walk all files under quartzOutput/<topicSlug>/ (no symlinks)
       For each .html file:
         - Compute depth (number of path segments relative to topic root)
         - Call rewriteHtml(content, topicSlug, depth)
         - Add to ZIP at path stripped of topic-slug prefix
           e.g. quartzOutput/password-managers/bitwarden/index.html → bitwarden/index.html
  4. Copy quartzOutput/static/ into ZIP at static/ (skip symlinks)
  5. Finalize ZIP

rewriteHtml(htmlContent, topicSlug, depth)
  Uses cheerio. prefix = "../".repeat(depth)

  a) Static asset paths (src/href starting with /static/):
     /static/foo.js → {prefix}static/foo.js

  b) Within-topic page links:
     href="/topicSlug/sub-page" → href="{prefix}sub-page/index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/index"   → href="{prefix}index.html"
     href="/topicSlug/"        → href="{prefix}index.html"

  c) Cross-topic page links:
     href starts with "/" but not with "/<topicSlug>/"
     → remove href attribute
     → add title="External topic (not included in export)"
     → add class="export-external-link"

  d) Anchor-only (#section), mailto:, relative paths → unchanged
```

### Step 3 — New file: `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js`

Small Node `http` server (no Express needed):

```
createExportServer(port, quartzOutput, vaultPath)
  Listens on 0.0.0.0:port (internal Docker port, not exposed to host)

  Routes:
    GET /export/<slug>
      1. Validate slug: reject if not /^[a-z0-9-]+$/
      2. Check slug in getPublishedTopics(vaultPath) → 404 if not found
      3. Check concurrency guard → 429 if already building this slug
      4. Call buildTopicExport(quartzOutput, slug, res)

    All other paths → 404
```

### Step 4 — Wire HTTP server into `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js`

After `configureQuartz()` and before the chokidar watcher:

```javascript
const { createExportServer } = require('./http-server');
createExportServer(3001, QUARTZ_OUTPUT, VAULT_PATH);
logger.info('Export HTTP server listening on :3001');
```

### Step 5 — Update `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf`

Add a proxy block inside the existing `server {}`:

```nginx
location /export/ {
    proxy_pass http://rockybot-bridge:3001/export/;
    proxy_read_timeout 30s;
    proxy_buffering off;
}
```

Notes-web (nginx) and the bridge are on the same Docker network and can reach each other by service name.

### Step 6 — Add export links to the generated root index in `quartz-builder.js`

Change the `topicLinks` generation:

```javascript
// Before:
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => `- [[${t}/index|${t.replace(/-/g, ' ')}]]`).join('\n');

// After:
const topicLinks = topics.map(t => {
  const label = t.replace(/-/g, ' ').replace(/\b\w/g, c => c.toUpperCase());
  return `- [[${t}/index|${label}]] · [⬇ Export ZIP](/export/${t})`;
}).join('\n');
```

### Step 7 — `docker-compose.yml`: expose bridge's port 3001 internally

Add to the `rockybot-bridge` service (no host port mapping needed — nginx reaches it by service name):

```yaml
rockybot-bridge:
  expose:
    - "3001"
```

---

## Critical Files

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `services/obsidian-bridge/package.json` | Add `archiver`, `cheerio` |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/export-builder.js` | **New** — ZIP generation + HTML rewriting |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/http-server.js` | **New** — HTTP API with slug validation + security gate |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/index.js` | Start HTTP server on port 3001 |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/src/quartz-builder.js` | Add export links to root index generation |
| `services/obsidian-bridge/nginx.conf` | Add `/export/` proxy_pass block |
| `docker-compose.yml` | Add `expose: ["3001"]` to bridge service |
| `docs/WORKFLOW.md` | Document the export feature |
| `CHANGELOG.md` | Add entry |

---

## Verification

1. Start the stack locally with `docker compose up`
2. Add a topic with `publish: true`, wait for Quartz build
3. Open `http://localhost:8080` — verify a "⬇ Export ZIP" link appears next to the topic
4. Click the link — browser should immediately start downloading `<topic>-export.zip`
5. Extract the ZIP and open `index.html` in a browser (file://)
6. Verify:
   - Topic index renders with Quartz styling (CSS/JS from local `static/`)
   - Sub-page links navigate correctly between pages in the ZIP
   - Cross-topic links render as non-clickable text with tooltip
7. Security spot-checks:
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/../etc/passwd` → 404
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/unpublished-topic` → 404
   - `curl http://localhost:8080/export/` → 404

name: Publish Docker Images

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      packages: write

    strategy:
      matrix:
        include:
          - service: bot
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot
            dockerfile: services/bot/Dockerfile
            context: .
          - service: obsidian-bridge
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-bridge
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-bridge/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-bridge
          - service: obsidian-headless
            image: ghcr.io/mrwong/rockybot-headless
            dockerfile: services/obsidian-headless/Dockerfile
            context: services/obsidian-headless

    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Log in to GHCR
        uses: docker/login-action@v3
        with:
          registry: ghcr.io
          username: ${{ github.actor }}
          password: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

      - name: Docker metadata
        id: meta
        uses: docker/metadata-action@v5
        with:
          images: ${{ matrix.image }}
          tags: |
            type=ref,event=branch
            type=semver,pattern={{version}}
            type=semver,pattern={{major}}.{{minor}}
            type=raw,value=latest,enable={{is_default_branch}}
            type=raw,value=edge,enable=${{ github.ref == 'refs/heads/dev' }}

      - name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}
        id: build
        uses: docker/build-push-action@v5
        with:
          context: ${{ matrix.context }}
          file: ${{ matrix.dockerfile }}
          push: true
          tags: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.tags }}
          labels: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.labels }}

      - name: Notify Discord (success)
        if: success()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -sL -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")
          echo "Discord HTTP $HTTP_STATUS: $(cat /tmp/discord-resp)"

      - name: Notify Discord (failure)
        if: failure()
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
        run: |

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
    tags: ['v*']

jobs:
  build-and-push:
    name: Build and push ${{ matrix.service }}

      - name: Docker metadata

          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0
          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -sL -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"✅ rockybot/${{ matrix.service }} published\",\"description\":\"Image: \`${{ matrix.image }}:${{ github.ref_name }}\`\\nRef: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`\",\"color\":3066993,\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")

name: Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  image:
    name: Docker image permissions
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Build bot image
        run: docker build -f services/bot/Dockerfile -t rockybot-test .
      - name: Verify HOME=/home/ubuntu is writable for uid 1000
        # Claude Code creates lock files and temp files in HOME. If the directory
        # is owned by root (the pre-1.0.1 behaviour), both subscription billing
        # and API key mode silently exit 0 with no output.
        run: |
          docker run --rm -u 1000:1000 -e HOME=/home/ubuntu rockybot-test \
            sh -c 'touch /home/ubuntu/.write-test && rm /home/ubuntu/.write-test && echo "PASS"'

  unit:
    name: Unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

  integration:
    name: Integration tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only run when explicitly enabled — set ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true in repo variables
    if: ${{ vars.ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install Claude CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run integration tests
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_CI }}
          CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE: 'false'
          VAULT_PATH: /tmp/ci-test-vault
          RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD: '0.10'
          AMEND_BUDGET_USD: '0.05'
        run: npm run test:integration

  notify:
    name: Notify Discord
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [image, unit, integration]
    if: always() && github.event_name == 'push'
    steps:
      - name: Post result
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          IMAGE_RESULT: ${{ needs.image.result }}
          UNIT_RESULT: ${{ needs.unit.result }}
          INTEGRATION_RESULT: ${{ needs.integration.result }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0

          FAILED=""
          [[ "$IMAGE_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}image "
          [[ "$UNIT_RESULT"  == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}unit "
          [[ "$INTEGRATION_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}integration"
          FAILED="${FAILED% }"  # trim trailing space

          if [[ -z "$FAILED" ]]; then
            TITLE="✅ rockybot tests passed"
            COLOR=3066993
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          else
            TITLE="❌ rockybot tests FAILED: ${FAILED}"
            COLOR=15158332
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          fi

          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -sL -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"${TITLE}\",\"description\":\"${DESC}\",\"color\":${COLOR},\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")
          echo "Discord HTTP $HTTP_STATUS: $(cat /tmp/discord-resp)"

file.content:
name: Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  image:
    name: Docker image permissions
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Build bot image
        run: docker build -f services/bot/Dockerfile -t rockybot-test .
      - name: Verify HOME=/home/ubuntu is writable for uid 1000
        # Claude Code creates lock files and temp files in HOME. If the directory
        # is owned by root (the pre-1.0.1 behaviour), both subscription billing
        # and API key mode silently exit 0 with no output.
        run: |
          docker run --rm -u 1000:1000 -e HOME=/home/ubuntu rockybot-test \
            sh -c 'touch /home/ubuntu/.write-test && rm /home/ubuntu/.write-test && echo "PASS"'

  unit:
    name: Unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

  integration:
    name: Integration tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only run when explicitly enabled — set ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true in repo variables
    if: ${{ vars.ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install Claude CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run integration tests
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_CI }}
          CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE: 'false'
          VAULT_PATH: /tmp/ci-test-vault
          RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD: '0.10'
          AMEND_BUDGET_USD: '0.05'
        run: npm run test:integration

  notify:
    name: Notify Discord
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [image, unit, integration]
    if: always() && github.event_name == 'push'
    steps:
      - name: Post result
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          IMAGE_RESULT: ${{ needs.image.result }}
          UNIT_RESULT: ${{ needs.unit.result }}
          INTEGRATION_RESULT: ${{ needs.integration.result }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0

          FAILED=""
          [[ "$IMAGE_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}image "
          [[ "$UNIT_RESULT"  == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}unit "
          [[ "$INTEGRATION_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}integration"
          FAILED="${FAILED% }"  # trim trailing space

          if [[ -z "$FAILED" ]]; then
            TITLE="✅ rockybot tests passed"
            COLOR=3066993
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          else
            TITLE="❌ rockybot tests FAILED: ${FAILED}"
            COLOR=15158332
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          fi

          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -sL -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"${TITLE}\",\"description\":\"${DESC}\",\"color\":${COLOR},\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")
          echo "Discord HTTP $HTTP_STATUS: $(cat /tmp/discord-resp)"

message.content: actually rather than run the test suite locally, can we run the test suite in docker for isolation? is this even a good idea?

lastPrompt: actually rather than run the test suite locally, can we run the test suite in docker for isolation? is this even a good idea?

content.text:
Yes, it's a solid idea. The approach is simple — no custom Dockerfile needed, just use the official `node:20` image with a volume mount:

```bash
docker run --rm \
  -v "$(pwd):/app" -w /app \
  node:20 \
  sh -c "npm install && npm run test:integration"
```

`node:20` is Debian-based so `unzip` is already there. The volume mount means no image rebuild when you change test files — just re-run the command. The only downside is ~2-3s of `npm install` overhead each time, which is fine for an integration suite.

Worth doing: add it as an npm script so it's a one-liner. Want me to wire that up and revert the CI bridge job (since tests will be validated locally instead)?

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

message.content: what about making a docker compose for an already baked integration env and then using that image to run the integration tests.

content.text:
Good call. The key insight is splitting deps (baked into the image, rebuilt only when `package.json` changes) from source (volume-mounted, always live). Here's the design:

**`Dockerfile.test`** (at repo root):
```dockerfile
FROM node:20-slim
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y unzip && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
WORKDIR /app
COPY package.json package-lock.json* ./
RUN npm install
```

**`docker-compose.test.yml`**:
```yaml
services:
  tests:
    build:
      context: .
      dockerfile: Dockerfile.test
    volumes:
      - .:/app
      - /app/node_modules   # anonymous volume keeps the baked node_modules
    command: npm run test:integration
```

Run with: `docker compose -f docker-compose.test.yml run --rm tests`

The `node_modules` anonymous volume is the key trick — it prevents the host's empty `node_modules` directory from shadowing the image's baked-in one.

First run builds the image and installs deps. Subsequent runs just start a container and mount your source — no `npm install` overhead. Only rebuilds when `package.json` changes.

Want me to implement this? I'd also add an npm script (`test:integration:docker`) as a shorthand and revert the CI `bridge` job since we'd validate locally.

hookInfos.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code stop'

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Tests

on:
  push:
    branches: [main, dev]
  pull_request:

jobs:
  image:
    name: Docker image permissions
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - name: Build bot image
        run: docker build -f services/bot/Dockerfile -t rockybot-test .
      - name: Verify HOME=/home/ubuntu is writable for uid 1000
        # Claude Code creates lock files and temp files in HOME. If the directory
        # is owned by root (the pre-1.0.1 behaviour), both subscription billing
        # and API key mode silently exit 0 with no output.
        run: |
          docker run --rm -u 1000:1000 -e HOME=/home/ubuntu rockybot-test \
            sh -c 'touch /home/ubuntu/.write-test && rm /home/ubuntu/.write-test && echo "PASS"'

  unit:
    name: Unit tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run unit tests
        run: npm test

  bridge:
    name: Bridge integration tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install system deps
        run: sudo apt-get install -y unzip
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run bridge integration tests
        run: npm run test:integration

  integration:
    name: Integration tests
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    # Only run when explicitly enabled — set ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS=true in repo variables
    if: ${{ vars.ENABLE_INTEGRATION_TESTS == 'true' }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
      - name: Install Claude CLI
        run: npm install -g @anthropic-ai/claude-code
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install
      - name: Run integration tests
        env:
          ANTHROPIC_API_KEY: ${{ secrets.ANTHROPIC_API_KEY_CI }}
          CLAUDE_SUBSCRIPTION_MODE: 'false'
          VAULT_PATH: /tmp/ci-test-vault
          RESEARCH_BUDGET_USD: '0.10'
          AMEND_BUDGET_USD: '0.05'
        run: npm run test:integration

  notify:
    name: Notify Discord
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: [image, unit, bridge, integration]
    if: always() && github.event_name == 'push'
    steps:
      - name: Post result
        env:
          DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL: ${{ secrets.DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL }}
          IMAGE_RESULT: ${{ needs.image.result }}
          UNIT_RESULT: ${{ needs.unit.result }}
          BRIDGE_RESULT: ${{ needs.bridge.result }}
          INTEGRATION_RESULT: ${{ needs.integration.result }}
        run: |
          [[ -z "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" ]] && exit 0

          FAILED=""
          [[ "$IMAGE_RESULT"  == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}image "
          [[ "$UNIT_RESULT"   == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}unit "
          [[ "$BRIDGE_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}bridge "
          [[ "$INTEGRATION_RESULT" == "failure" ]] && FAILED="${FAILED}integration"
          FAILED="${FAILED% }"  # trim trailing space

          if [[ -z "$FAILED" ]]; then
            TITLE="✅ rockybot tests passed"
            COLOR=3066993
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          else
            TITLE="❌ rockybot tests FAILED: ${FAILED}"
            COLOR=15158332
            DESC="Branch: \`${{ github.ref_name }}\`"
          fi

          HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -sL -w "%{http_code}" -o /tmp/discord-resp \
            -X POST "$DISCORD_WEBHOOK_URL" \
            -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
            -d "{\"embeds\":[{\"title\":\"${TITLE}\",\"description\":\"${DESC}\",\"color\":${COLOR},\"url\":\"${{ github.server_url }}/${{ github.repository }}/actions/runs/${{ github.run_id }}\"}]}")
          echo "Discord HTTP $HTTP_STATUS: $(cat /tmp/discord-resp)"
